steps.title: Use the parallel lines property
steps.content: Lines q and r are parallel, and both are intersected by transversal s. The angle 77° is formed at the intersection of s and q, while the angle $y°$ is formed at the intersection of s and r. These angles are co-interior angles (also called same-side interior angles), which means they lie on the same side of the transversal and between the parallel lines.

steps.title: Apply the co-interior angle theorem
steps.content:
When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, co-interior angles are supplementary—they add up to 180°. Therefore:

$$77° + y° = 180°$$

Solving for $y$:

$$y° = 180° - 77° = 103°$$

steps.title: Substitute into the equation and solve for x
steps.content:
We are given that $y = 2x + 7$. Substituting $y = 103$:

$$103 = 2x + 7$$
$$96 = 2x$$
$$x = 48$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
